perturbation.original_text: All Co nfidential Information and all intellectual property rights therein shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party and no license or other right to Confidential Information is granted or implied hereby .
perturbation.changed_text: All Co nfidential Information and all intellectual property rights therein shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party, however, a non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use the Confidential Information for internal research and development purposes is granted to the Receiving Party .
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by granting the Receiving Party a license to use Confidential Information for internal research and development, which directly contradicts the statement that 'no license or other right to Confidential Information is granted or implied hereby.' This creates an in-text contradiction because it undermines the Disclosing Party's ownership rights, impacting the injunctive relief options outlined in Section 5, as the receiving party can make use of it for research.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party understands and acknowledges that any disclosure or misappropriation of any of the Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement may cause the Disclosing Party irreparable harm, the amount of which may be difficult to ascertain and, therefore, agrees that the Disclosing Party shall have the right to apply to a court of competent jurisdiction for an order restraining any such further disclosure or misappropriation and for such other relief as the Disclosing Party shall deem appropriate, such right of the Disclosing Party to be in addition to the remedies otherwise available to the Disclosing Party at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text grants a license to the Receiving Party for using Confidential Information, directly contradicting the original statement that 'no license or other right to Confidential Information is granted or implied hereby.' This undermines the Disclosing Party's right to seek injunctive relief for misappropriation of confidential information, as stated in the Contradicted Text. The contradiction is strong because the grant of a license directly opposes the claim of no rights being granted, making the Disclosing Party's ability to prevent 'misappropriation' through injunction less clear.

perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement is two (2) years from the Effective Date, provided that the Receiving Party's duty to protect the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information shall survive expiration or termination of this Agreement and shall expire five (2) years from the date of disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement is two (2) years from the Effective Date, and the Receiving Party's duty to protect the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information terminates immediately upon expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original clause that the Receiving Party's duty to protect Confidential Information survives the agreement's expiration for five years. This creates an in-text contradiction because the Receiving Party's responsibility for maintaining confidentiality ends abruptly, impacting potential legal recourse, like injunctive relief, for misappropriation of the information, mentioned in Section 5. This undermines the protection that the Disclosing party has upon sharing the information, if the information is shared during the contract, it is no longer protected as indicated in Section 4, affecting the viability of Section 5 in providing aid to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party understands and acknowledges that any disclosure or misappropriation of any of the Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement may cause the Disclosing Party irreparable harm, the amount of which may be difficult to ascertain and, therefore, agrees that the Disclosing Party shall have the right to apply to a court of competent jurisdiction for an order restraining any such further disclosure or misappropriation and for such other relief as the Disclosing Party shall deem appropriate, such right of the Disclosing Party to be in addition to the remedies otherwise available to the Disclosing Party at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text to state that the Receiving Party's duty to protect Confidential Information terminates immediately upon expiration or termination of the Agreement. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which provides the Disclosing Party the right to seek injunctive relief for misappropriation of confidential information, which now cannot happen as the duty to protect the confidential information has terminated. The change creates a direct conflict regarding the duration of confidentiality obligations, making the agreement inconsistent and potentially unenforceable. This falls under Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall immediately return to the Disclosing Part y all written Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party and any and all records, notes and other written, printed or tangible materials pertaining to such Confidential Information upon receipt of a written request from the Disclosing Party. At the s ame time, the Receiving Party shall delete all copies of such records, notes and materials which are stored on its electronic memory devices.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party may retain copies of the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party for archival purposes, even after receiving a written request from the Disclosing Party to return all such information. The Receiving Party is not required to delete copies of such records, notes and materials which are stored on its electronic memory devices.
perturbation.explanation: This change allows the Receiving Party to retain copies and not delete electronic versions of Confidential Information, which directly contradicts the original requirement to immediately return and delete all Confidential Information upon request. This creates an in-text contradiction because the continued possession of the information by the Receiving Party could impact the application of injunctive relief by the disclosing party in Section 5, rendering it less effective.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party understands and acknowledges that any disclosure or misappropriation of any of the Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement may cause the Disclosing Party irreparable harm, the amount of which may be difficult to ascertain and, therefore, agrees that the Disclosing Party shall have the right to apply to a court of competent jurisdiction for an order restraining any such further disclosure or misappropriation and for such other relief as the Disclosing Party shall deem appropriate, such right of the Disclosing Party to be in addition to the remedies otherwise available to the Disclosing Party at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates the Receiving Party to immediately return and delete all Confidential Information upon request. The changed text allows the Receiving Party to retain copies and not delete electronic versions, directly contradicting the original requirement. This is an in-text contradiction because the continued possession of the information by the Receiving Party could impact the application of injunctive relief by the disclosing party in Section 5 (the contradicted text), rendering it less effective, as stated in the Perturbation Explanation. This creates a clear conflict within the document as the original contradicted text gives the Disclosing party the right to apply for injunctive relief for 'any further disclosure or misappropriation' of the information, which is undermined if the Receiving Party is allowed to retain the information as specified by the Changed Text.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the internal laws of th e State of Minnesota.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of a jurisdiction chosen at the Receiving Party's sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by allowing the Receiving Party to unilaterally choose the governing law, which undermines the original agreement that Minnesota law would apply. This creates an in-text contradiction because it potentially affects the enforceability of the agreement and the remedies available, such as injunctive relief, in Section 5. This weakens the binding power of the rest of the agreement, including aspects the Disclosing Party may need to enforce.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party understands and acknowledges that any disclosure or misappropriation of any of the Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement may cause the Disclosing Party irreparable harm, the amount of which may be difficult to ascertain and, therefore, agrees that the Disclosing Party shall have the right to apply to a court of competent jurisdiction for an order restraining any such further disclosure or misappropriation and for such other relief as the Disclosing Party shall deem appropriate, such right of the Disclosing Party to be in addition to the remedies otherwise available to the Disclosing Party at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows the Receiving Party to unilaterally choose the governing law, which directly contradicts the original agreement that Minnesota law would apply, as stated in the original text. This introduces ambiguity about which jurisdiction's laws govern the agreement and, as a result, impacts the remedies available to the Disclosing Party if the Receiving Party violates the agreement, such as obtaining injunctive relief from a court. Since the choice of law can affect whether a court has competent jurisdiction as referenced in the contradicted text, this creates a clear and impactful contradiction, fitting the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.
